description: Patterns that must NOT appear in public-tier files. The public tier is reusable across organizations — specific company names, customer names, internal URLs, GitHub org slugs, and tenant identifiers belong in knowledge/confidential/{org}/. Allowlisted terms are legitimate industry vocabulary (e.g. 'SBI Model' = Situation-Behavior-Impact feedback framework) or placeholder templates.

denied_patterns.name: internal-atlassian-subdomain
denied_patterns.rationale: Org-specific Atlassian (Jira / Confluence) subdomain. Replace with ${ATLASSIAN_BASE_URL} and resolve in confidential/{org}/.

denied_patterns.name: internal-tld
denied_patterns.rationale: Internal-only DNS suffix. Replace with a placeholder and resolve in confidential tier.

denied_patterns.name: aws-account-id
denied_patterns.rationale: AWS account id embedded in an ARN. Move to confidential/{org}/endpoints.json.

denied_patterns.name: splunk-hec-url
denied_patterns.rationale: Splunk Cloud tenant URL. Replace with ${SPLUNK_HEC_URL} and resolve in confidential tier.
